perturbation.original_text: A. In consideration for the services to be provided, AVDU agrees that it will remit the agreed upon stock certificate within five (5) days of both parties executing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. In consideration for the services to be provided, AVDU agrees that it will remit the agreed upon stock certificate within thirty (30) days of both parties executing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that AVDU will remit the stock certificate within five days of executing the agreement. By changing this to thirty days, we create a conflict with the term sheet (Exhibit A) which specifies a different payment schedule. This inconsistency makes it unclear when the payment is actually due, creating potential for dispute and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation: In consideration for providing these Services, AVDU shall pay UTK $120,000 worth of unregistered shares of common stock (31,413 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the stock certificate would be remitted within five days of execution. This was changed to thirty days. The contradicted text states that the payment (in the form of stock shares) will be made 'upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement.' The change from five days to thirty days directly conflicts with the original agreement that payment is due at the time of execution. This makes the contract inconsistent, because it is unclear when the payment is actually due, which could lead to a dispute. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: 'Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**.'

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 15 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a 30-day written notice for termination. The term sheet also indicates a 30-day notice period. Changing this to 15 days creates a direct contradiction within the agreement and with Exhibit A, causing confusion about the required notice period for terminating the agreement and creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term: The term of the Agreement will be for 12 months unless terminated sooner. This Agreement may be renewed upon mutual, written agreement of the parties. Either party may terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a 30-day written notice for termination. The changed text alters this to a 15-day notice. The contradicted text explicitly states 'Either party may terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.' The change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the required notice period, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as it's unclear whether 15 or 30 days' notice is required.

perturbation.original_text: AVDU will not seek to acquire any technologies presented to AVDU by UTK directly from the technology developer for a period of 24 months following the termination of this Strategic Alliance agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU will not seek to acquire any technologies presented to AVDU by UTK directly from the technology developer for a period of 12 months following the termination of this Strategic Alliance agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a 24-month restriction period during which AVDU cannot acquire technologies presented by UTK directly from the technology developer after the agreement terminates. By changing this to 12 months, we create a conflict with Section IX (C) which specifies that C. AVDU agrees that for a twenty four months (24) following the execution of this Agreement, AVDU shall not, without UTEK's prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for employment any present employee of UTEK, or request induce or advise any employee of UTEK to leave the employ of UTEK. In turn, UTEK agrees that it will not directly or indirectly solicit any present employee of AVDU. This inconsistency makes it unclear to the court system, when is the actual time period in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. AVDU agrees that for a twenty four months (24) following the execution of this Agreement, AVDU shall not, without UTEK's prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for employment any present employee of UTEK, or request induce or advise any employee of UTEK to leave the employ of UTEK. In turn, UTEK agrees that it will not directly or indirectly solicit any present employee of AVDU.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the restriction period from 24 months to 12 months during which AVDU cannot acquire technologies from a developer if introduced by UTK after termination of the agreement. The contradicted text states a 24-month restriction for soliciting employees. This creates a direct conflict as to what the agreed-upon time period actually is, as it makes it unclear whether the time frame is 12 months or 24 months.
